item.title: Could you say no?
item.description: French runner Alice Finot got down on one knee and proposed to her boyfriend after smashing a record in her Olympic race. She proposed using the Olympic pin she was wearing that said “Love is in Paris” “I told myself that if I ran under nine minutes, knowing that nine is my lucky number and that we’ve been together for nine years, then I would propose." Finot finished in 8 minutes, 58.67 seconds. I tend to dislike public proposals, but this one … Source: https://www.threads.net/@jasminericegirl/post/C-d0nmnywBe/?xmt=AQGzHNawga-FGG2YsAvZrf1wt9SM5qAnS5SYldEVaBVXrg
item.dc:creator: Huor

item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
i just think it's neat
as a fellow glasses wearer i appreciate this
something about a button..
good for him
i've been called out once again
i guess
another personal attack
i see no other possibilities. bert it is
we've all been there
or is it?
as is tradition
mmm hmm
2018 discipline was vastly different than 2019 and especially 2020
now listen here sonny jim
i'm totally cool with just weed
good to know?
that's my set.. have a great weekend gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Sportsmanship right there! Women supporting women!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: AITA?
item.description: I was raised quaker, and despite becoming atheist I still hold to the values. I abhor violence, but I also hate bullies more. Saw some guy in a cut people off at a gas station with a line just so he could pump first. I walked over, he rolls down his window and I call him out and tell him to apologize. He tells me to get out of his face and kicks his door open at me, cutting my leg open and knocking me back. I respond by grabbing the door and p*nch him in the face, he falls back into his seat and I just ... leave? I feel disgusted with myself, even today. I know this will die in usersub, I just have to get it off my chest
item.dc:creator: PAHamster

item.title: Tonight the entire women's basketball team of the local university strolled into the bar at my favorite Mexican restaurant, all dressed to kill... and yeah, I just sorta died.
item.description:
Forgot all about that guac, not gonna lie. I pretty much keeled right over. My wife, who's 5'11", just rolled her eyes and threw tortilla chips at me.
*sighhhhhhh*
item.dc:creator: wootusmagootus

item.title: We?ve never had a president that has committed this amount of heinous crimes, and republicans act like it?s insane when we try to hold him accountable for those crimes.
item.description: Every time I hear them say shit like this ?we?ve never indicted a former president? I?m like uh ya exactly, that should tell you something but I don?t think it?s telling you what it should be telling you. (originally posted by @CapnAdorable on 2023-08-10 22:42:14)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: Screaming into the void
item.description:
I don't blame him, really. His divorce was finalized, he didn't have anything tying him down, bought a new car with a rooftop tent, and decided to take a year off to travel and decide what he actually wants from life. Oh...and our boss denied his annual scheduled pay increases not once, but twice.
Full disclosure--my immediate coworkers are awesome, but management/environment is super toxic with all the usual signs (statistically high turnover, harassment investigations, questionable budget practices, etc.). I assumed the bulk of my former colleagues' duties without complaint and with minimal training.
So instead of hiring a straight replacement, my boss restructures the department entirely & creates a new analyst classification. New position is salaried with a $7k/yr pay increase. Also an extra week of PTO.
I submit application, reference letter, writing sample, budget projections, mock board presentation...the works. Panel interviews this week.
I'm a little disappointed, but not entirely surprised. The $ would've been nice, but potentially not worth the headache. I was up against external candidates with MBAs, more experience etc.. New role would've been required to work after hours, play politics with elected officials, review 25,000+ accounts, & work directly with 4 department managers (only one of whom is a genuinely good leader AND good person).
I know it's possible to make no mistakes and still lose. But saying "we're hiring somebody else rather than promoting you, now go train them how to do the job" is extra tone deaf, right? I'll try not to hold it against the newbie...they have no idea what's coming for them.
Since my boss restructured the department, they effectively eliminated any other potential advancement opportunities. I'll max out of current classification pay scale in 3 years...so unless things drastically change I'll have no reason to stay beyond that.
All things considered, I'm fortunate. I don't have an SO or kids to support, I'm debt free & I make enough to pay the bills. My employer currently fully funds our healthcare (that's on the chopping block next year *cue union whispers*), there's a match to retirement contributions, I get every other Friday off, & I'm eligible for pension after 5yrs. It isn't a demotion...it's just become a defacto dead-end.
She's seen all the shenanigans and is eligible for early retirement in 3 years. She, too, was passed over for promotion prior to my hiring (technically, she should've gotten our supervisor's job...but nepotism is a thing). Between the two of us, we run all aspects of payroll, benefits, billing, purchasing, accounts payable/receivable. Nobody else on staff--including our boss--knows how those systems work. I'm not usually one to burn bridges, but oh how satisfying it would be for both of us to leave simultaneously and watch the implosion from the rear view mirror.
Cat tax. TLDR: Didn't get the job, didn't really want the job. Still kinda bummed. *rant over* Thanks for the entertainment, usersub. Hope y'all get to do something fun this weekend. MV edit: Thanks for the fake internet points. Be excellent to eachother.
item.dc:creator: oneduringwanderer

item.title: Smol Shrimp is the cutest little tripawwd
item.description: Shrimp is available adoption from the good folks at Seattle Animal Shelter
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Faraday had no formal math education and worked all these out by observation and graphs
item.description: Faraday never published any grant mathematical theorems like Maxwell, but his observation and graphs were so on point, even today, introduction to electromagnetism starts with his work.
item.dc:creator: Cliperinoposterino

item.dc:creator: RElGNMAN

item.title: This meathead life saver will be crossing the rainbow bridge tonight or tomorrow morning
item.description:
This meathead's name is Bacchus. We rescued him when he was 1 1/2 years old. In this pic, he is about 15. He is a total "Momma's Boy!"
He has been a very Good Boi for 16 years with us! He even learned to sniff out and inform me about the high and low blood sugars I was about to experience. Taught himself to be a diabetic alert dog when I didn't have the advanced tech to do that job.
In March of this year, he experienced sneezing, coughing, and a bloody nose. The vet saw a swelling and decided to perform a nasal flush.
That flush showed an unmovable blockage in his right nasal cavity. We could be aggressive with biopsies and ultrasound - which would have been very hard on him - or wait and see.
We chose to wait and see
Within a few months, it was apparent that the growth was aggressive. And cancerous. To treat would be invasive and cause this elderly gentlemen a great deal of anxiety and pain. So we started palliative care.
We have been cooking him batches of rice, soft dog food, and no-salt-added vegetable mixes. He has been getting all the attention, butt scratches, and treats he can take.
Last night, I noticed that the tumor had started to impact his right eye deeply. This morning, he had a head tic. An involuntary pull to the left that is a sign of neurological impact. He is disoriented and has poor motor control. He didn't wolf down his breakfast like normal. The swelling turned into a major lump over his eye. We can not touch his face for pain. We contacted those who may wish to say goodbye.
If he makes it through tonight, he has an appointment in the morning. We refuse to force him to suffer. Bacchus, you were and are the Goodest Boi! Void: Thank You for receiving my broken heart. This is the reality of bringing a pet into your life....and it never gets easier. BUT, The good outweighs the bad! This is not an FP edit, just an edit to say Thank You! to those that have reached out. I GREATLY appreciate your kindness and care. You are comforting me, GREATLY! Losing a pet of more than a decade and a half is always going to be difficult. Having a community that hugs you, physically or virtually, is cathartic. I APPRECIATE YOU! This IS an FP update: He has been visited by those most dear to him. And he has received a great deal of love and care tonight. We are hesitant about his prognosis tomorrow, but we have every confidence that he will have a smooth sailing to Valhalla, where he belongs. Bacchus has been the mightiest warrior against type 1 diabetes and deserves every cup of mead at the tables. TO VALHALLA!! TO VICTORY AND HONOR THAT YOU DESERVE!! Final Update: As of 9 am CDT, Bacchus sauntered into the Halls of Valhalla after slipping away from his momma's arms with the assistance of his favorite vet. Thank You Imgurians for sharing this pain with me.
item.dc:creator: grandopening

item.title: Tidal Bore.
item.description: The Qiantang river's tidal bore is the world's largest and most powerful of its kind. Sometimes two tides meet together forming a shape like a cross, that can become even more captivating at sunset.
item.dc:creator: iceage2012
